channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Beijing loyalists will vote to endorse Macao's former top judge as next leader of the casino hub
item.description: About 400 Beijing loyalists will vote to endorse the sole candidate for the top job in the Chinese city of Macao in a largely ceremonial election that would make him the first leader of the casino hub to be born in mainland China.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-Scottish First Minister Alex Salmond, who nearly got Scotland's independence from UK, dies at 69
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Alex Salmond, the former first minister of Scotland who for decades championed Scotland’s independence from the U.K. and nearly accomplished it, has died. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: Palestinians in northern Gaza are describing heavy Israeli bombardment in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy And Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Poland's leader plans to suspend the right to asylum as country faces pressure on Belarus border
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's leader said Saturday that he plans to temporarily suspend the right to asylum as part of a new migration policy, pointing to its alleged abuse by eastern neighbor Belarus and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hindus in Bangladesh celebrate their largest festival under tight security following attacks
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Schoolteacher Supriya Sarker is glad to celebrate Bangladesh's largest Hindu festival of Durga Puja but feels the festivities would be more jubilant without the fear and violence that overshadow this year's event.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM and Al EMRUN GARJON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian officials say 22 killed in Israeli airstrikes
item.description: The Israeli military has renewed its orders for Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip to leave their homes and shelters as troops continue a weeklong offensive against militants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK leader Keir Starmer is marking 100 days in office. It has been a rocky ride
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer marks 100 days in office on Saturday with little cause for celebration.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Navy SEALs' drownings in Iranian op were preventable, investigation finds
item.description: An investigation into the death of two Navy SEALS who drowned during a mission to seize Iranian missile parts bound for the Houthi rebels in Yemen found that the deaths could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon in crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israeli airstrikes hit the headquarters of the U.N. peacekeeping force and other positions in southern Lebanon in recent days, drawing widespread condemnation from around the world.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson says Biden told him his family origins 'were not really Irish at all'
item.description: Boris Johnson, former United Kingdom prime minister, said President Biden told him he was not Irish, according to his new memoir "Unleashed," which was published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Activists risk their lives to rescue animals in areas of Lebanon hit by Israeli airstrikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hours after an Israeli strike destroyed a three-story building in Beirut, killing at least 10 people, Maggie Sharawi received a telephone call from a person living nearby saying that the attack had killed a cat that had several kittens.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea accuses South Korea of flying drones to its capital and threatens to attack next time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea has accused rival South Korea of flying drones to its capital to drop anti-North Korean propaganda leaflets and threatened to respond with force if such flights occur again. South Korea issued a vague denial of the allegation.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkish citizens fleeing Lebanon mourn the homes and family left behind
item.description: ON BOARD THE TCG SANCAKTAR (AP) — Eyup Sabri Kirgiz gathered up his loved ones — both family and pets — and with a heavy heart left his beloved city of Beirut behind, after two weeks of deadly airstrikes that had traumatized his family.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken tells ASEAN the US is worried about China's 'dangerous' actions in disputed sea.
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken told Southeast Asian leaders Friday that the U.S. is concerned about China's “increasingly dangerous and unlawful” activities in the disputed South China Sea during an annual summit meeting, and pledged the U.S.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's national airliner accused of flying weapons to Beirut airport for terror proxy Hezbollah: Report
item.description: Iran’s domestic airline, already sanctioned for support to Russia, is being accused of aiding Hezbollah by an Iranian dissident group who has called for additional sanctions to be put on the carrier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gender of female captain not to blame for sinking of $61M navy ship: New Zealand defense minister
item.description: New Zealand's Defense Minister has rebukes claims that the appointment of a female to captain a $61 million navy ship ultimately led to its sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens wounded, at least 22 killed in airstrikes in Beirut as tensions escalate in Israel conflict with Iran
item.description: At least 22 people were killed and dozens of others left wounded in airstrikes in central Beirut, further escalating Israel’s deadly conflict with Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Susan Rice: Trump's reported calls with Putin appear illegal
item.description: Former White House adviser and United Nations ambassador Susan Rice called out former President Trump over a report that he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin at least seven times since leaving office, suggesting such calls would violate the Logan Act.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: John Legend calls for Harris to put 'conditions' on US aid to Israel
item.description: Music superstar John Legend is calling for Vice President Harris to make U.S. aid to Israel conditional, amid its ongoing offensives in Gaza and Lebanon. In an interview with Zeteo’s Mehdi Hasan, Legend said America must use its aid to Israel as “leverage.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, The Hill

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former hostage wants ‘peace in the world’ one year later
item.description: One year later, the Chicago-area woman is steadfast in her faith and hopeful others captured that day will be released.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Iran’s missiles strike Israel, and a deadly terror attack in Tel Aviv
item.description: On the same day Iran attacked Israel with some 181 missiles last week, a terrorist attack saw 7 innocent Israelis murdered in cold blood by two Palestinians in the Tel Aviv area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel-Hamas war will affect generations: Humanitarian worker
item.description: Since Israel's offensive began after the October 7 attacks, the death toll in Gaza is estimated at 41,800, according to the Palestinian Health Authority.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
